Petition For Suspension Of Powers And Removal Of Person Represebtative
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Continued To Jul-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caroline Hinshaw for Elizabeth Holmes, Administrator (via Zoom).
Mark Johnson for Michelle Holmes, Objector (via Zoom).

The Court inquired about the absence of the Administrator, Elizabeth Holmes. Ms. Hinshaw represented that Ms. Holmes was not present but was aware of the hearing. Mr. Johnson stated that the citation issued to the Administrator had not been served because he was unable to locate her. Ms. Hinshaw agreed to accept service on the Administrator's behalf, and also consented to service by email. The Court directed Mr. Johnson to have a new citation issued accordingly.

Mr. Johnson addressed the Court regarding the lack of an accounting despite multiple continuances since the petition's filing in December 2023. Ms. Hinshaw indicated she had retained a local accounting firm in Rocklin and requested six weeks to complete the accounting. Mr. Johnson objected to a further continuance, citing repeated delays and inadequate record production. He requested immediate suspension of the Administrator's powers and issuance of an order to show cause for her removal. He alleged that documents provided were disorganized, repetitive, and insufficient to produce a proper accounting. He further noted that there may be concerns regarding compensation received by the Administrator without court authorization. Ms. Hinshaw acknowledged the delays but stated that her client had attempted to negotiate a waiver of accounting in order to distribute the estate and reduce costs.

After hearing arguments, the Court ordered the issuance of an Order to Show Cause/Order to Appear to Administrator Elizabeth Holmes, as to why she should not be suspended, and set the matter for hearing on July 23, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. The Court directed that the accounting be filed and served by July 9, 2025. The Court stated that if an accounting is not produced by the given deadline, the Administrator will be suspended and a further Order to Show Cause will be set as to why they should not be removed.

All underlying matters were also continued be heard on July 23, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Appearances: Caroline Hinshaw for Elizabeth Holmes.
Jerard Hendrickson (Eviction Attorney) for Elizabeth Holmes.
Elizabeth Losa in Propria Persona.

The Court noted that the matter was continued from the last hearing for the purpose of Ms. Losa filing her objections and obtaining Counsel, and that nothing has been filed by Ms. Losa. Ms. Losa stated that she sent a letter and presented oral objections with respect to the Administrator taking funds out of the bank accounts. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Ms. Losa has no standing, that the issue of the funds has already been ruled upon and denied, that they need to gain access to the property and that the Decedent died four years ago. Ms. Hinshaw also stated that she filed a declaration in response to the Examiner's notes with respect to the entries of payments to Mr. Hedrickson's firm and provided an endorsed copy of the declaration. The Court overrules Ms. Losa's objections. The Court granted the Petition for Authority to Further Engage Attorneys in Real Property Law Matters (subject to review of the order).

Ms. Losa stated that she had an Attorney (Ernest Der), that Mr. Der informed her that he was no longer representing her and that he did not give her a sensible response as to why. Ms. Losa also stated that the Decedent was her 40 year plus partner, sole mate and common law husband, that she will be filing a claim, that Mr. Der violated her rights, that she needs representation and that she needs an extension. The Court stated that no claim has been filed, that there is nothing before it, that Ms. Losa needs to file something and that she needs an Attorney to do so. Ms. Losa expressed concern about the amount of funds that have been paid out, stated that some of those funds are hers and that they (opposing side) need to pay for her Attorney's fees. Ms. Hinshaw stated that Ms. Losa has no standing. Mr. Hendrickson stated that there is a pending unlawful detainer matter, that there are six defendants, that four are unrepresented and fighting it, that the matter is ready to be set for trial, that $20,000.00 is owed and that they have had some settlement discussions. Ms. Losa stated that she would like to have the probate matter resolved first. Ms. Hinshaw stated that she has not billed for all of the extraordinary services and explained what services she has provided. The Petition to Approve Status of Administration and the Petition for Authority to Further Engage Attorney's in Real Property Law Matters are under submission.
